section.heading: Adverse action

subsection.heading: First Violation.—
subsection.chapeau: If the Director of the Office of Personnel Management determines, after notice and an opportunity for a hearing on the record, that an employee of an agency has violated section 9202, the Director shall—

paragraph.content: issue to the employee a written warning that includes a description of the violation and the additional penalties that may apply for subsequent violations; and
file such warning in the employee’s official personnel record file.

subsection.heading: Subsequent Violations.—
subsection.chapeau: If the Director of the Office of Personnel Management determines, after notice and an opportunity for a hearing on the record, that an employee that was subject to subsection (a) has committed a subsequent violation of section 9202, the Director may take the following action:

paragraph.content: For a second violation, suspension of the employee for a period of not more than 7 days.
For a third violation, suspension of the employee for a period of more than 7 days.

subparagraph.content: suspension of the employee for a period of more than 7 days; and
a civil penalty against the employee in an amount that is not more than $250.
suspension of the employee for a period of more than 7 days; and
a civil penalty against the employee in an amount that is not more than $500.
suspension of the employee for a period of more than 7 days; and
a civil penalty against the employee in an amount that is not more than $1,000.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 116–92, div. A, title XI, § 1122(a), Dec. 20, 2019, 133 Stat. 1606.)
